© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Can you imagine starting a business with virtually no assets, doing a Seed Launch
to a tiny list (made up almost entirely of your personal contacts), and quickly growing a
business that did a half-million dollars in sales? This is a truly remarkable Case Study,
and I think you’re going to get a lot out of it.
It tells the story of Tara and Dave Marino, who started their business with a Seed
Launch for a product that didn’t exist yet… and generated six sales. That was the
relatively humble start that set Tara and Dave up for a rocket ride which included moving
their family from California to France (which fulfilled Tara’s lifelong dream) and
generating over a half million dollars in sales.
This is certainly not a “typical” or “average” story or Case Study. Tara and Dave
invested in themselves, they invested in Product Launch Formula, and they even invested
in one of my high-level live training events. And they took massive action, even when they
weren’t 100% sure – following Tara’s “ready-fire-aim” approach.
IMPORTANT: you will see that they continually leveraged their results –
building on each success and stepping up to play a bigger game.
This was a wonderful interview, and I feel blessed to be part of their incredible
story and to celebrate their huge success. And I know you will find this Case Study both
extremely instructional and incredibly inspiring.
Enjoy… and learn!
- Jeff Walker
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Jeff: Hi everyone. This is Jeff Walker and I am really excited today to have Tara and
Dave Marino on the line. They are actually calling in from France, they have an
amazing story to share with you. Dave and Tara, welcome. I’m glad to have you on
the phone.
Tara: Thank you, Jeff. It’s good to be here.
Dave: Yes, it’s awesome. Thank you for having us.
Jeff: This is going to be great, because what you’ve done is so remarkable. We met for
the first time back in I think 2009. I’m going to say it was the summer of 2009.
Can you give us the quick “where you were then and what happened and where
you are now.” Give us the synopsis, and then we’ll just dive right in.
Dave: Well Tara was starting this crazy online business and I was working in a cubicle
for a company. It was about my 10th year working for this company.
Tara was starting this online business and she’s starting to look at programs and
whatnot and I’m trying to figure out how to put an eBook online; what that even
means, how do I deliver it, and all that kind of stuff.
I learned what Alexa was and I looked at the “Hot Movers” for the day; and there
was something called Product Launch Formula. I clicked on it, and then I opted
in. The next day I made my first ever online purchase for an information program
I bought the $2,000.00 Product Launch Formula.
I said, “Babe, I just bought this program. It looks really good. I’m excited.”
Tara was completely supportive. “Go for it”…but for me it was definitely a stretch.
Tara: That was really a big deal. I want to comment on that because this was the first
time I had seen Dave come alive. He had been in a cubicle for 10 years and I really
wanted to get into this online world and really produce something amazing in the
world.
Jeff, you and I have talked about that before. What we call “Internet Marketers”

or any kind of online business, is so foreign to the outside world. What it is that

I had seen many brick and mortar companies and seen the kind of profit that is
available there and I wasn’t interested in that. I really wanted to do something

When I saw Dave excited about a product online I was 100% behind it because up
until that point it had been me kind of pulling him. “Come on, I know there is
something here in this online world that we can do together.”
As soon as he found Product Launch Formula he was in.
That was the beginning and from the knowledge we gained from Product Launch
Formula it really did give us the confidence to step out on our own.
I know without the strategy and without the steps that are within the Product
Launch Formula we would not have had the confidence to step into this the way
we did. In a matter of less than three years we created a company of over a half
million dollars. And we also moved to France. And I’m starting a lingerie line.
Everything that we were dreaming about back when Dave was in the cubicle has
actually come to fruition.
Jeff: That’s amazing. Dave you say you were in a cubicle; you’ve been there 10 years. One
thing you didn’t mention was by almost anyone’s standards this was a really good
job you had. It wasn’t a dead end job. You were doing real well there.
Dave: Yes, I was pulling in six figures for I think seven or eight years in a row. I was
doing well. It was in a secure job, at least I guess you could call it that. Most
people thought I was crazy when I gave my resignation. People thought I was nuts
- but it felt right, and it was right.
Jeff: That is awesome. What were your first steps? How did you get started?
Dave: First steps were we actually did a small Seed Launch to our little list of something
like 200 people. It was really small when we did “You’re Perfect”. It was a great
learning experience. I think we had something like three or four people buy.
Tara: Six.

Dave: Okay, so we gave it to one person for free and we sold to four or five. It was messy.
We didn’t have a plan. I think our prelaunch was three or four weeks. It just kept
on going. We just weren’t following the plan that was laid out for us.
Tara: No, but you know what was great about that is that I remember saying I want to
charge $500 for the six week program and all of my colleagues, even those that
were in the online world at that time, were saying, “Tara, you're crazy. That’s too
much. You can’t do that.”
Back then, no one was charging $500. They were charging $97, $147, $297.
But I believed in my work. And we had the Product Launch Formula. We had
created some prelaunch content. Did we learn and do we do different launches
now? Yes. But you know what? We did it. We pulled the trigger.
One of the things I can remember you saying is that there are some people that
are aim-aim-aim and there are other people that are then fire-aim. We had the
combination here. I’m definitely the “fire-aim” and Dave is the “aim-aim-aim” so
we had a decent combination of both and we actually did it. We did our first
launch.
Our first mini-launch was internal. It was like $3,000.00. That was just a mini
test one. Then what happened, Dave?
Dave: Then we focused on some one-on-one coaching for a while. We built up some
internal programs and did that for a while. In September of 2010 we did our first
big launch I would say.
Tara: How many people?
Dave: Our list was 1,000 total people and we sold 57 units of a brand new program
which at the time was $200 so it was just under $12,000 launch on the front end.
Then we did another launch to the buyers.
Tara: Yes, so we launched to our buyers and that created over six figures for us. So our
first big launch was an over six-figure launch.
Jeff: Wow, so if you could see me right now I’m just grinning ear to ear listening to
this. I love this. This is fantastic. It’s so fun.
I want to go a little deeper. The very first launch that brought in approximately

$3,000, that one you said you had this list of about 200 people. Where did that
list come from?
Tara: Oh, this is the best because people always ask me how I got started; what did I do.
It came from my personal email address book. Whoever was in my personal email
box and whatever friends I had on Facebook. We launched to them. At that point
we didn’t have a real list. We were just starting to build it.
Dave: We had a few organic Google-type stuff but not 200 people. One-hundred-fifty of
them were your contacts and we probably had 50 people that came from
somewhere else like Twitter.
Tara: I think that someone had actually featured one of my newsletters somewhere so
we got some organic traffic that way but it was hardly any.
Jeff: So you started publishing. You sent out at least one newsletter it sounds like;
maybe more than one. You started publishing to your contacts, the people in your
inbox, then you organically just by word of mouth added another 50ish people so
you had 200 people.
It sounds like a lot of first launches where it sounds like you evolved as you went
along and it wasn’t as tightly choreographed as you probably do now… but you
did this organic launch process and boom - $3,000.
What was that product?
Tara: It was called “You’re Perfect” and it was really the basis of our next product which
is “The “Power of Sensuality””. That became our baseline foundational product.
We learned from the first group of people in our first Seed Launch that the name
“You're Perfect” didn’t really resonate or explain the value of the program. So we
ended up developing it further into “The “Power of Sensuality”” which is now our
baseline product.
Jeff: When you delivered that program was the product already complete? Or did you
do calls? Did you create the product as you delivered it?
Tara: That’s the way that I really love to do everything. I make it as I go. I had an idea
and an outline of what I was going to deliver. I had six key steps that I wanted to
go over with them over a period of six weeks, so I did six live calls over six weeks.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Every week they got templates that I created that week about the process and
what it was that they were going to be experiencing next.
Every week we had a live call, they received their templates, they went through
their templates, and then we moved on to the next week. We delivered it over six
weeks.
By the time I was done I had worksheets, I had templates, and we had the
recordings. We just went in after the program, refined them, built them up… and
we had “The “Power of Sensuality”” program ready to roll out for our next big
launch.
Jeff: That’s perfect. That’s a classic Seed Launch that you leveraged into a major asset,
a major brand.
Tara: And it was FUN, Jeff. I want to say that because I have so many of my clients now
that say, “Oh, I’ve heard about this launch thing and how do I do it?” They are all
overwhelmed and they’re trying to figure out all the pieces.
When you can create one of these launches, and you're really engaged in the
material and engaged with your clients, the process does unfold really organically
and it’s fun. It’s enjoyable. You're getting to know your clients. Your clients are
getting to know your material. You're growing your material at the same time. It’s
one of the most beautiful ways to build your business.
I’m not saying that launches can’t get stressful. You can get stressed if you choose,
but there is a way to really do a launch that is elegant and really in alignment with
the gift you want to share with the world. At the end of the day that is what it’s
about. We all of course want to create prosperity but we also want to have an
impact.
Jeff: That’s great. Tara, when you say that it’s funny because that is the word I use to
describe these launches - elegant. In fact, that’s one of my favorite words in the last
couple years. Of course that’s your word too.
Tara: We resonate that way.
Jeff: One of my other favorite words for the last couple years is grace.
Tara: That’s what we say, “With grace and ease.” You really can do it that way.

Jeff: I have a really important question for you here. This is specifically for you Tara.
This is something a lot of people that will be listening right now will be stuck
with… and that’s the confidence to do this.
You put this program together, you mailed friends, family, and everyone in your
inbox… you got a few hundred people on your list, and you just decided you were
going to do this. You had an outline and you just did it.
But where did the confidence come from? Where did that “I’m going to be able to
deliver this. I’m going to sell this thing and I’m going to create a great experience. I
know I can do it.”
Where did that come from?
Tara: I love this question because in my work with women now this is something that
always comes up for them. How do we tap into the confidence?
I will say that I think it comes from a couple of different places. First of all, we had
the strategies and the knowledge from Product Launch Formula. When you
have some sort of training behind you it does create a sense of structure and a
sense of confidence.
I’m not saying that everybody needs to go out there and get degrees after their
name; in fact, I don’t believe in that at all. However, I do believe in allowing
yourself to receive some training and some sort of structure, some guidelines that
you can follow along. We had that, so that immediately set a place of confidence.
As far as the delivery of the program this is what I can say: I believe in what I’m
doing so deeply, so wholeheartedly, in the gift and the message that I have to
share with women. If I can get a little bit spiritual maybe for a second, I felt
accountability to my soul that these women trusted me, that they invested in this
product, and that I knew I was going to show up. I knew that even if nobody
bought the program that I was going to show up and deliver it.
Dave: You said that you were going to do that and I actually said, “What?” She said, “I’m
doing the program. I’m doing it on Tuesday nights; every Tuesday night for the
next six weeks I’m doing it. I don’t care if anybody is on there.”
I think originally I thought “I’m not sure if we should do this.” We only have a 200
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

person list, and that came from Tara’s address book. But Tara said, “No, we’re

It’s the launch process and then it’s the delivery and if you can be so connected
with your message and your material that you want to deliver it no matter what, it
does something to your confidence and it does something to the energy of the

The confidence came from a couple different places: knowing I had the strategies

It’s an evolutionary process for me. That is something I’m committed to.
Jeff: That’s great. Since you are so articulate about this I’m just going to ask one more

Tara: You got it!

And it’s not like they give degrees out in sensuality. It’s not like you have a

Did you ever have the experience of people saying, “Who are you to teach this?
Why should you teach this?”
Or did you ever ask that of yourself? Who am I to teach this?
I only ask because I know all of those things can be roadblocks for people…
Tara: I feel that we all at some point in time ask that question. “Who am I to do this?”

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
I actually did a whole telesummit on “Who am I not to be?” I feel like what really
resonated with me as far as sensuality is I had that reflection my entire life that I
was beautiful, sensual, in line with my truth and I finally started to accept it for
myself.
If anybody out there is listening and saying, “I don’t know if I have the skills to do
that.” You need to just settle into your truth and realize that you have something
unique that you want to share with the world and if you can engage in a sensual -
my term -- sensual relationship with that, if you can give yourself permission…
then just share it. Just go for it.
No one else is going to give you permission to do it. If you're waiting to get the
letters by your name so that you have the stamp of approval to do this, then
you're going to be waiting forever. No one else can give you permission to do it…
you have to give yourself permission.
I would say if you have a message, if you have something you want to share… then
live it. Breathe it. Go through the launch process and share it. Share it no matter
what.
Jeff: That’s a great message. It’s fantastic.
So you did this as a Seed Launch - you did “You're Perfect” in six weeks. You got
on the phone and you taught it. I’m sure you learned an incredible amount in the
teaching, and you learned how to teach it throughout those six weeks.
Were there any surprises in the process? What was the impact on the final
product of doing it that way?
Tara: I did some Q&A during the six weeks of “You're Perfect”. The impact of seeing
what the women actually responded to, the questions that they were asking was
really impactful. Sometimes we think that there is something that people need to
know, and they either already know it or they don’t care about it.
Then when you actually start interacting with the material and you hear the
questions they’re asking and, oh my gosh! One simple shift that you shared with
them, and all of the sudden that is what everybody is talking about. Whatever
those pieces were is exactly what we turned into “The “Power of Sensuality””
program. It was the foundation.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Dave: One of the things that Tara taught was that there were daily requirements for the
women to live sensual lives. If I remember right, that was something Tara was
doing but it was kind of like, “I’m going to throw this into the program”, and all of
the sudden it’s a daily practice years later that we are still teaching and we
incorporate into all our events and all of our programs.
Jeff: That is so cool. The name, “The “Power of Sensuality””, did that come out of what
you learned in the teaching?
Tara: The “Power of Sensuality”, the name, was a process in itself because I was kind of
hesitant to fully embrace and embody that term “sensuality”. I thought, “Oh my
gosh, what are people going to think of it? They’re going to think its sexuality. It’s
not that.”
The feedback from our clients was how do I live this way? How do I live my life
alive and awake?
What I was starting to teach, without fully being aware of it, was living alive and
awake with all of your senses. I would ask women, “How do you want to feel?
What do you want this to taste like? What do you want this to look like?”
Once I started interacting with that myself I thought, “Oh my goodness, it’s true. I
actually assist women in living more sensual lives.” It came out of the
conversations with them during that first class.
None of them actually said to me, “You're a sensuality expert” or “You're teaching
me sensuality”, but it was really an awakening for me. And the big release of it
was actually coming out and sharing with the world that this is what you are
looking for. This is what you’ve been asking for. It’s actually sensuality. That’s why
you don’t have the relationship with money; that you don’t have the relationship
you're seeking in your life.
It’s because you don’t have a sensual relationship with that aspect. It developed
really organically and I finally surrendered to it.
Jeff: You walked forward from that Seed Launch and you said you did some one-on-
one coaching in there. I imagine just having done that Seed Launch would
probably give you the kind of positioning and confidence to be able to sell that
one-on-one coaching fairly effectively.

Tara: I would say it was about eight months.
Jeff: That timeline tracks pretty close to when I did this with Product Launch Formula.

Before we leave it, could you just quickly tell the story of moving to France?

Tara: I’ll share a little bit and then Dave, if you want to add something. Ever since I was
a little girl I wanted to live in France. It’s been a dream of mine. It’s not something
that just appeared out of the blue. It’s something that I had been intending and

When Dave and I got married he kind of not so secretly always told me I was his

When we really started to grow the business, after we had our first mini launch,

13
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
That summer we went to France on vacation and as we were landing in Nice I
looked over at my two boys, Dominic and Nikko, and I started crying and Dave
turned around and said, “Oh my gosh, we’re not going home, are we?”
We had already put everything in storage because we were planning on moving
into a different house when we got back to California. Well, we landed for a
vacation and we didn’t go home. The only time we went back to California was to
get our visas.
We had the confidence knowing that we could create money. We knew that we
had already done it and it was just the beginning. We had that confidence that we
could launch, that we could grow our business, that we could create income from
anywhere in the world. That is exactly what we’ve been looking for.
We didn’t wait for anyone else to give us permission. We said, “All right, let’s do it
now.” We’re not going to wait until we’re retired and the kids are in college. We’re
not waiting. We’re doing this.
And now we’re on our third year in France.
Dave: By the way, I didn’t speak French. I’m getting better but I’m still not as good as I
could be. That is one of the downfalls of working online is that you could just go
hide… go get your baguettes and go home and work a little and just enjoy.
Jeff: That’s great.
So getting back to hardcore marketing here, let’s talk about the launch of “The
Power of Sensuality” and how that differed from your first Seed Launch.
Obviously the product is different. I assume you had been building your presence
in the market and your list. Let’s talk about that launch a little bit.
Dave: Well we got one or two Joint Venture (JV) partners on board. We had a complete
video series scripted out and nicely edited with music and transitions. That was
how we chose to do it. It was still not a very large list – about 1,000 people.
We had 57 people buy on our first launch of that product, and around $12,000 in
sales.
Jeff: So you made around $12,000. That’s $12 per email address, which is extremely
solid. Of those 1,000 subscribers, how many came from your JV partners?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 18 --

14
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Dave: We had one JV who added probably 300 or 400 to our list, which was huge for us
at the time. That was like an extra half of our list just got added, which was pretty
awesome.
Our partnership with that JV was very strategic - it was a perfect affiliate for us
because it was somebody who had the same beliefs as we did, but taught
something completely different. Her list was more in the health and wellness
niche and we were more in the mindset and personal growth niche. It just worked
out perfectly. It was a perfect message without being exactly the same.
Jeff: How did you end up landing that JV? Was that someone you met at a conference
or that had seen your work?
Tara: I know you know who she is: Rose Cole. I met her at a conference and we just
immediately hit it off. I see this with you and how organically your relationships
have transformed with Frank and Eben. It was the same for me and Rose.
I didn’t go in looking for a JV. We actually met outside the bathroom; so typical
woman to woman. She was looking for something in her business and I was able
help her with that. That was how the relationship formed.
When you can align yourself with JVs from that standpoint, where it just happens
to be your friend… you connect, you support each other, and you believe in what
the other one is doing. That’s when a JV partnership not only makes sense but
also feels good.
Jeff: That is exactly the way to do it. People always want the “easy button” for JVs and
they always want a ton of JVs, but in reality you don’t need a lot of them. You
need one or two or a maximum of five. The way it works, in my experience, is
you build a relationship first. Then you promote for each other.
Tara: I think people really underestimate the power of JVs. And they really
underestimate what it really is. It’s a relationship like any other relationship. You
want quality. You're not looking for quantity. You need to be aware of who you're
interacting with and why.
Everybody can feel when you're just going after “I just want you to mail for me.”
It’s a relationship like any other relationship. You don’t want to take it for
granted.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Jeff: So 57 sales and you're feeling pretty good about this. That’s almost 6% of your list
buys, which in any measure is fantastic. Then you said you ended up doing
another launch to the buyers. That was what really took this to a totally different
level.
Dave: We had a bonus module at the backend which was honestly a last minute
decision. True Tara style. She was teaching the Beauty Formula which ended up
becoming a whole new program. We offered the bonus as a teleclass, and at the
end of the call she offered a deeper level of training.
That was huge. That was what filled in the backend.
Jeff: This is the power of a list. I talk about this a lot - that having a big list isn’t as
important as having a responsive list. Even a small list can do wonders.
Also, the difference between a buyer’s list and a prospect list: a buyer’s list is
always far more powerful than a prospect list. I tend to figure that person on your
buyers list will give you 15 times the result that a new prospect is worth.
So essentially, in this second round you had a list of 57 to sell to, right?
Dave: Yes.
Jeff: Of those 57, in addition to being able to serve them better and take them deeper
and deliver more value… what kind of sales did you generate?
Dave: It was over $90,000.00 between the one-on-one clients and the Beauty Formula;
Jeff: That 57 person list of buyers was worth another $90,000!
I don’t have my calculator handy but that’s a lot of money for a small number of
clients. That goes to show the value you created for them and the impact you
made on them because there is no way you could generate that kind of revenue or
those kind of sales from someone unless they are happy. That’s just
extraordinary.
Dave: It’s over $1,500 a person.
Jeff: That is just unbelievably amazing. And the fact that you did it by the seat of your
pants, that’s really great. Ready-fire-aim tends to win in this business… so
congratulations.

Dave: Then we built the new program on the fly, and that program has now become a

Tara: Then we did another launch and sold about 67. From those two lists of buyers, we

Jeff: So out of that 120-person universe you put 37 in a room. Then you made another
$190,000 in sales. Amazing. Unbelievable.
Just to make it clear, since you're living in France, you're primarily selling in the
United States, right?
Tara: Yes.
Dave: The U.S., the UK. English speaking countries.
Jeff: So it’s probably 90% U.S., 5% Canada, 4% UK, and 1% the rest of the world.
Dave: Exactly right. Australia a little bit but yes, that’s exactly right.
Jeff: I’m so glad we talked. So I’m sure everyone is curious.. can you share a little bit
more now about what your life is like, what your business is like, what the
business has evolved into.
Dave: When I was in a cubicle we were looking for a solution and a business before we
knew about online business. We were looking for a business and we were looking
for ways that we could see the world. As a kid I didn’t get to see the world that
much. I grew up in one town and kind of stayed there.
Now we’re going to London in three days. We just got back from the U.S. We flew
Tara’s parents out to watch the kids so they could be with them. We went to Bora
Bora. We’re traveling and thinking, “Where do we go next? Italy?” So we’re
traveling and seeing the world in a way that was not even remotely possible.
We have this amazing message that we’re continuing to get out deeper and deeper
and to more and more people and impacting families and lives.
Tara: We’re continuing to evolve as people. The business supports us in a way that we
get to step into our desires and our dreams and continue to grow. We’re creating
more programs within Elegant Femme. We have our “Essential Entrepreneur”

which is the next big program for us – we’re going to be launching it in a couple of

I’m now creating a lingerie line, which is one of my biggest dreams other than

Like Dave said, we get to travel the world with our kids. We were in Paris last
week with my parents and the boys have been to Italy. They’ve been so many
places with us.
I remember when Dave used to get up and go to his corporate job. He would leave
the bed at 5:30 in the morning and he would come home at 7:30 or 8:00 at night.
I remember looking at him one morning and saying, “This is ridiculous. We
married each other so we could be together; so we could see each other. Not so we
could just try to recover our relationship on the weekends.”
Now we have that. We’re together every day and it’s the same with our kids. They
come home and we’re here. We get to see them. We get to be with them.
Is there a balancing act when you're an entrepreneur? Yes, there absolutely is. But
we don’t have the excuse of “I’m in the office all day”. We have an opportunity to
really spend our lives the way that we want to.
Jeff: That’s so cool.
Tara: Thank you for that, Jeff.
Jeff: You're welcome. I love this. I really do. I love making an impact. I love seeing lives

incredibly positive impacts in the world.
It’s what I call the “Ripple Effect” and I’m sure you have your version of that,
because you're very much doing the same. I know you are incredibly passionate
about the people that you help and the people that they go on to help.
Tara: Yes. I love them. I don’t know any other way to say it. I’ll get off the phone

so deeply connected with the women who say yes to themselves and allow
themselves to invest in themselves through us.
